pattern.title: Fillers: tota, niinku, siis
pattern.summary: Real speech runs on small words that buy thinking time and add tone: 'tota' (um), 'niinku' (like), 'siis' (I mean / seriously), 'no' (well). They disappear in writing, but you won't sound - or understand - natural Finnish without them.
examples: Tota... mä en oo ihan varma. = Um... I'm not quite sure.
Se oli niinku tosi outoo. = It was like really weird.

glosses.siis: I mean / so - here it signals disbelief

sentences.fi: Tota... mä en oo ihan varma.
sentences.written: Tuota... minä en ole ihan varma.

glosses.tota: um / uh - spoken 'tuota', buys thinking time

glosses.niinku: like - filler from 'niin kuin', dropped in writing

sentences.en: Well, what do we do then?

glosses.no: well - starts a reply or a decision
glosses.mitäs: what - the -s makes it casual and friendly

sentences.en: Um, could you help me a bit?
sentences.written: Tuota, voisitko sinä auttaa minua vähän?

sentences.fi: Se on niinku paras kahvila ikinä.
sentences.en: It's like the best café ever.

glosses.niinku: like - filler from 'niin kuin', dropped in writing

sentences.en: I mean, I love this place.

glosses.siis: I mean - mid-sentence it adds emphasis

sentences.fi: Se oli vaan jotenki hassu juttu.
sentences.en: It was just kind of a funny thing.
sentences.written: Se oli vain jotenkin hassu juttu.
